back_translation: And the Canaanite, king of Arad, dweller of the Negev, heard that Israel had come along the road of Atharim; and he fought against Israel, and he captured from him captives.
And Israel vowed a vow to the LORD and said: 'If You will surely give this people into my hand, I will devote their cities to destruction.'
And the LORD heard the voice of Israel and gave up the Canaanite; and he devoted them and their cities to destruction; and he called the name of the place Hormah.
And they journeyed from Mount Hor by the way of the Red Sea, to go around the land of Edom; and the soul of the people became short on the way.
And the people spoke against God and against Moses: 'Why have you brought us up from Egypt to die in the wilderness? For there is no bread and no water, and our soul loathes this wretched bread.'
And the LORD sent among the people the fiery serpents, and they bit the people; and a great number of people from Israel died.

And the people came to Moses and said: 'We have sinned, for we spoke against the LORD and against you. Pray to the LORD, and let Him remove from us the serpent.' And Moses prayed on behalf of the people.
And the LORD said to Moses: 'Make for yourself a fiery serpent, and set it on a pole; and it shall be — everyone who is bitten and sees it shall live.'
And Moses made a bronze serpent, and set it on the pole; and it was — if a serpent bit a man, and he looked at the bronze serpent, he lived.
And the sons of Israel journeyed, and they camped in Oboth.
And they journeyed from Oboth, and camped in Iye-abarim, in the wilderness which is opposite Moab, from the east of the sun.
From there they journeyed, and camped in the Valley of Zered.

From there they journeyed, and camped on the other side of the Arnon, which is in the wilderness that goes out from the border of the Amorite; for the Arnon is the border of Moab, between Moab and between the Amorite.
Therefore it shall be said in the Book of the Wars of the LORD: 'Waheb in Suphah, and the wadis of Arnon,'
'and the slope of the wadis that extends to the dwelling of Ar, and leans against the border of Moab.'
And from there to Beer — it is the well of which the LORD said to Moses: 'Gather the people, and I will give to them water.'
Then Israel sang this song: 'Come up, O well; sing to it!'
'A well — princes dug it, nobles of the people hollowed it out with a scepter, with their staffs.' And from the wilderness — Mattanah.
And from Mattanah — Nahaliel, and from Nahaliel — Bamoth.
And from Bamoth — the valley which is in the field of Moab, the top of Pisgah; and it looks down on the face of the wasteland.
And Israel sent messengers to Sihon king of the Amorite, saying:
'Let me pass through your land; we will not turn into field or vineyard, we will not drink water of a well; by the King's Highway we will go, until we have passed through your border.'
And Sihon did not give Israel passage through his border; and Sihon gathered all his people, and went out to meet Israel into the wilderness; and he came to Jahaz, and he fought against Israel.
And Israel struck him with the edge of the sword, and took possession of his land, from Arnon to Jabbok, to the sons of Ammon; for the border of the sons of Ammon was strong.
And Israel took all these cities; and Israel dwelt in all the cities of the Amorite, in Heshbon and in all its daughter-towns.
For Heshbon was the city of Sihon king of the Amorite; and he fought against the former king of Moab, and took all his land from his hand, as far as the Arnon.
Therefore the poets say: 'Come to Heshbon; let it be built, and let the city of Sihon be established.'
'For fire went out from Heshbon, a flame from the city of Sihon; it consumed Ar of Moab, the masters of the high places of Arnon.'
'Woe to you, Moab! You have perished, people of Chemosh! He gave his sons as refugees, and his daughters in captivity, to the king of the Amorite, Sihon.'
'And we have shot them down; Heshbon has perished as far as Dibon; and we have demolished as far as Nophah, which reaches to Medeba.'
And Israel dwelt in the land of the Amorite.
And Moses sent to spy out Jazer, and they captured its daughter-towns, and he dispossessed the Amorite who was there.
And they turned and went up the way of Bashan; and Og king of Bashan came out to meet them, he and all his people, for battle at Edrei.

And the LORD said to Moses: 'Do not fear him, for into your hand I have given him, and all his people, and his land; and you shall do to him as you did to Sihon king of the Amorite, who dwells in Heshbon.'
And they struck him and his sons and all his people, until there was no remnant left for him; and they took possession of his land.
